dublinCore.dc:title: 119 HR 2683 : Remote Access Security Act
dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

form.current-chamber: IN THE SENATE OF THE UNITED STATES

action.action-desc: Received; read twice and referred to the Committee on Banking, Housing, and Urban Affairs

form.official-title: To provide for control of remote access of items under the Export Control Reform Act of 2018.

section.text: This Act may be cited as the Remote Access Security Act.

section.header: Control of remote access of items under the Export Control Reform Act of 2018
section.text: The Export Control Reform Act of 2018 is amended as follows:

paragraph.text: In section 1742 (50 U.S.C. 4801), by adding at the end the following:

The term remote access means access on a purposeful, knowing, reckless, or negligent basis to an item subject to the jurisdiction of the United States under this Act by a foreign person through a network connection, including the internet or a cloud computing service, from a location other than where the item is physically located if the Secretary determines that the use of the item could pose a serious risk to the national security or foreign policy of the United States. Nothing in this paragraph may be construed to lower the requisite mens rea required to be proven for criminal liability under section 1760.

clause.text: in subparagraph (A), by inserting or remote access of such items after export of items; and
in subparagraph (B), by inserting or remote access of such items after export of items; and
in the matter preceding subparagraph (A), by striking and in-country transfer of items and inserting in-country transfer, and remote access of items; and
in subparagraph (A), by inserting or remote access after the release.
in paragraph (1), by striking and at the end;
in paragraph (2)(F), by striking the period at the end and inserting ; and; and
by adding at the end the following:

paragraph.text: the remote access of items subject to the jurisdiction of the United States by a foreign person.

clause.text: in paragraph (6), by striking and at the end;
in paragraph (7), by striking the period at the end and inserting ; and; and
by adding at the end the following:

paragraph.text: regulate the remote access of items described in subsection (a)(3).

clause.text: by striking or in-country transfer each place it appears and inserting in-country transfer, or remote access; and
by striking subsections (b)(1) or (b)(2) and inserting subsections (b)(1), (b)(2), or (b)(8).
in paragraph (3), by striking and in-country transfers and inserting in-country transfers, and remote access;
in paragraph (4), by striking and in-country transfers and inserting in-country transfers, and remote access;
in paragraph (5), by striking and in-country transfers and inserting in-country transfers, and remote access;
in paragraph (6), by striking United States export control and inserting United States control;
in paragraph (7), by striking export controls and inserting controls;
in paragraph (10), by striking or in-country transferred and inserting in-country transferred or accessed remotely;
in paragraph (11), by adding at the end before the semicolon the following: or remote access; and
in paragraph (15), by adding at the end before ; and the following: or remotely access (including the provision thereof);

subparagraph.text: in subsection (b), by striking or in-country transfer and inserting in-country transfer, or remote access; and
in subsection (d)(1)(A), by striking or in-country transfer and inserting in-country transfer, or remote access (including the provision thereof).

clause.text: in subparagraph (C), by striking and in-country transfers and inserting in-country transfers, and remote access (including the provision thereof); and
in subparagraph (E), by striking and in-country transfers and inserting in-country transfers, and remote access (including the provision thereof); and

subparagraph.text: in subsection (c), by striking export controls and inserting controls.
in subsection (a), in the matter preceding paragraph (1), by striking and in-country transfer and inserting in-country transfer, and remote access; and
in subsection (b), by striking or in-country transfer and inserting in-country transfer, or provide remote access to.
in subsection (a), by striking or in-country transfer and inserting in-country transfer, or remote access; and
in subsection (c)(2), by striking export controls and inserting controls.

clause.text: in clause (ii), by striking any export control document or any report and inserting any document or report; and
in clause (iii), by striking or in-country transfer and inserting in-country transfer, or remote access;

subparagraph.text: in subsection (c)(1)(C), by striking or in-country transfer and inserting in-country transfer, or remotely access (including the provision thereof); and

clause.text: in clause (i), by striking or in-country transfer outside the United States any item and inserting in-country transfer outside the United States any item or remotely access any item (or provide such access); and
in clause (ii), by striking or in-country transfer and inserting in-country transfer of items, or provide remote access to items.

subparagraph.text: in subsection (a)(5), by striking or in-country transferred and inserting in-country transferred, or remotely accessed; and
in subsection (h)(1)(B), by striking or in-country transfer and inserting in-country transfer of items, or provide remote access to items.

paragraph.text: In section 1767(b)(2)(A) (50 U.S.C. 4825(b)(2)(A)), by striking and in-country transfer and inserting in-country transfer, or remote access.

subsection.text: The Secretary of Commerce shall ensure that the appropriate congressional committees are kept fully and currently informed of any anticipated promulgation of regulations to control the remote access of items under the Export Control Reform Act of 2018, as amended by section 2, including ensuring such committees are informed, in a classified setting as necessary, on—

paragraph.text: the national security risk that would be addressed by the regulations;
how the method of the regulations addresses the national security risk; and
how the regulations may impact the economy of the United States.

subsection.text: Nothing in this section may be construed to require the approval of the appropriate congressional committees as a condition precedent to the exercise of an authority under the Export Control Reform Act of 2018, as amended by section 2.
In this section, the term appropriate congressional committees means—

paragraph.text: the Committee on Foreign Affairs of the House of Representatives; and
the Committee on Banking, Housing, and Urban Affairs of the Senate.

attestation-group.attestation-date: Passed the House of Representatives January 12, 2026.
